skill.name: domain-expertise-template
skill.description: Template for creating domain expertise skills with structured guidance, principles, processes, output templates, and intake questions.
skill.summary: Create structured domain expertise skills for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
skill.author: ChaiWithJai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backticks, a hidden home-directory path, and weak-crypto blocker patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are Markdown syntax, a user-facing installation example, and placeholder text; no command execution, cryptographic code, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution at SKILL.md:12
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 12 uses inline Markdown code for the path ~/.claude/skills/your-domain/SKILL.md. It is documentation text and does not execute a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown formatting in a documentation template. No executable Ruby, shell interpreter call, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution at SKILL.md:13
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 13 uses inline Markdown code around [PLACEHOLDER]. It is formatting for a replacement instruction and not a shell command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown formatting in a documentation template. No executable Ruby, shell interpreter call, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution at SKILL.md:19
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 19 starts a fenced Markdown example. It is documentation syntax and not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown formatting in a documentation template. No executable Ruby, shell interpreter call, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution at SKILL.md:99
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 99 starts a nested fenced Markdown output example. It is displayed text and not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown formatting in a documentation template. No executable Ruby, shell interpreter call, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backtick Command Execution at SKILL.md:110
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 110 closes a fenced Markdown example. It contains no interpreter call or command string.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is Markdown formatting in a documentation template. No executable Ruby, shell interpreter call, or user-controlled command path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden Home Directory Path at SKILL.md:12
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 12 shows a user-facing destination path under ~/.claude for copying a skill file. It does not read, write, conceal, or modify files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path appears only inside a setup instruction. There is no script, filesystem API, or automated file access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden File Access at SKILL.md:12
low_findings.description: TRUE EVALUATION: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 12 references ~/.claude in documentation, but no code performs hidden file access or persistence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only prose and Markdown on the cited line. No file operation, permission change, or persistence mechanism is present.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to turn expert methods into usable AI guidance. This template provides a structured skill format for principles, processes, outputs, and intake questions.
actual_capabilities: Provides a reusable SKILL.md structure for domain expertise skills.
Defines sections for overview, use triggers, key terms, and core principles.
Includes a process template with purpose, actions, and expected output.
Shows where to add output templates and common mistakes.
Includes intake question scaffolding for gathering user context.
limitations: It is a template, not a finished domain-specific skill.
Users must replace placeholders before practical use.
It does not validate the quality or accuracy of domain content.
It does not include scripts, tests, or automation.

use_cases.title: Document a Consulting Framework
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated consulting method into a structured AI skill with principles, process steps, and intake questions.

use_cases.title: Standardize Internal Guidance
use_cases.description: Create a consistent template for teams that want shared AI guidance across specialized business domains.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Marketplace Skill
use_cases.description: Draft a domain expertise skill that explains when to use it, what it produces, and what mistakes to avoid.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Domain Skill
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner author creating a first draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this template to create a skill for my domain. Ask me for the domain, target users, key outcomes, and common mistakes before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Fill the Framework Sections
prompt_templates.scenario: Author has a domain and wants structured content.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me complete the overview, when-to-use triggers, key terms, principles, and process sections for a domain expertise skill about [domain].

prompt_templates.title: Improve an Existing Draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my draft domain expertise skill. Identify vague triggers, missing principles, weak process steps, and unclear output templates.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt for Marketplace Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced author preparing a public skill.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this domain expertise skill for marketplace readiness. Check clarity, scope, evidence, safety boundaries, prompt injection risk, and user-facing value.

output_examples.input: Create a domain expertise skill for product discovery interviews.
output: A skill outline with trigger conditions for interview planning and synthesis.
Core principles covering customer evidence, bias reduction, and decision relevance.
A process section for planning, conducting, and summarizing interviews.

output_examples.input: Review my domain skill draft for gaps.
output: A short gap analysis covering unclear scope, missing intake questions, and incomplete output expectations.
Specific revision suggestions for principles, examples, and anti-patterns.
A readiness summary for publication or internal use.

output_examples.input: Turn my expert checklist into a reusable AI skill.
output: A structured skill draft with an overview, checklist-based process, common mistakes, and expected deliverables.
Plain-language prompts that help users provide the right context before work begins.

best_practices: Replace every placeholder with specific domain guidance before using the skill.
Define clear trigger conditions so the assistant knows when the skill applies.
Include concrete examples and anti-patterns that match real user workflows.
anti_patterns: Publishing the template without replacing placeholders.
Using broad domain claims without process steps or evidence standards.
Adding instructions that override system, security, or user consent requirements.

faq.question: Is this a complete domain expertise skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a template that authors must customize for a specific domain and audience.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it run commands or scripts?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The file is Markdown documentation and contains no executable scripts.

faq.question: Why did static analysis report command execution?
faq.answer: It matched Markdown backticks and fenced examples. Manual review found no actual command execution.

faq.question: What should authors add before use?
faq.answer: Authors should add domain triggers, principles, process steps, examples, output templates, and intake questions.

faq.question: Can this template be used for regulated domains?
faq.answer: Yes, but authors should add domain-specific safety boundaries, review requirements, and compliance limits.
